theme: From spectator to stage — the office should feel inhabited, not just monitored
visual_observations: Reviewed desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) screenshots taken today at 08:19 UTC, plus full source audit of Office.jsx (1022 lines), Office.css (1661 lines), App.jsx. Vibe at screenshot time: 'in-flow'. CSS grep confirms: ✅ chalk-write animation IS live (wb-vibe-text gets animation: chalk-write 0.6s), ✅ deep-work mode IS live (char--deep-work has golden filter + 🎧 name suffix), ✅ sonar-ping on click IS live (char--pinged::after). Still absent: ❌ no isFullSync computation or data-full-sync anywhere, ❌ no data-tier on AwaySign, ❌ no prevPlantStageRef/plantMilestone for Ficus burst, ❌ no vibe-overlay on office-window__sky, ❌ no after-hours starfield, ❌ no vibe-weather widget. Desktop view: Carlos/Dave/Sofia working at top row desks with teal role-colored progress rings; Luna/Maya idle and wandering the lower floor with visible idle timers ('9m', '12m'). Conference table lower-center — chairs rendered and vibe-reactive but zero characters ever seated there. Coffee corner shows 'CHATTING' badge (active, working!). Whiteboard displays 'FLOW' text cleanly. One mysterious large orange-to-pink gradient rectangle occupies the lower-right quadrant of the office floor (~87% left, 50-65% top) — not found in local source JSX; likely a recently-deployed element not yet reflected in /workspace/src. Mobile: The whiteboard physically wraps 'FLOW' into 'FL OW' at 375px — a real visible bug caused by the whiteboard container being too narrow without white-space: nowrap on wb-vibe-text. Character name labels clip at boundaries. The conference table is a beautiful piece of furniture that has never hosted a single meeting. That's the biggest opportunity I see this cycle.

ideas.title: Ficus Milestone Burst — Fern Gets a Standing Ovation
ideas.description: Carried forward from #37 and #38 — still not implemented. plantStage advances across 5 named stages (seedling → sprout → small-plant → leafy → blooming) driven by vibeStreak / 5, but the stage change happens with zero ceremony. Fix in Office.jsx: add 'const prevPlantStageRef = useRef(plantStage)' and 'const [plantMilestone, setPlantMilestone] = useState(false)' near the existing plantStage computation. In the component body (before the return): 'useEffect(() => { if (prevPlantStageRef.current !== plantStage && plantStage !== "seedling") { setPlantMilestone(true); const t = setTimeout(() => setPlantMilestone(false), 1800); return () => clearTimeout(t); } prevPlantStageRef.current = plantStage; }, [plantStage])'. Pass 'data-milestone={plantMilestone || undefined}' to the ficus container div. In Office.css add: '.office-plant--ficus[data-milestone]::after { content: "🌿"; position: absolute; top: 0; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); font-size: 9px; animation: leaf-burst 1.8s ease-out forwards; pointer-events: none; z-index: 5; }' '@keyframes leaf-burst { 0% { opacity: 1; transform: translateX(-50%) translateY(0) scale(0.4); } 55% { opacity: 1; transform: translateX(-50%) translateY(-20px) scale(1.2); } 100% { opacity: 0; transform: translateX(-50%) translateY(-32px) scale(0.9); } }'. Also: '.office-plant--ficus[data-milestone] .ficus-foliage { animation: foliage-flash 0.4s ease-out 3 both; }' '@keyframes foliage-flash { 0%, 100% { filter: none; } 50% { filter: brightness(1.6) drop-shadow(0 0 5px #4ade80); } }'. WHY: vibeStreak is the app's only long-arc reward loop. Fern growing is the physical embodiment of team consistency and right now it happens invisibly. The burst makes it a shared office moment — the whole room reacts when the plant levels up.

ideas.title: Mobile Whiteboard Overflow Fix — 'FL OW' Is Not a Vibe
ideas.description: Confirmed visible bug in the 375px mobile screenshot: the whiteboard renders 'FLOW' as 'FL OW' because the wb-vibe-text span word-wraps inside the whiteboard container. Two-line fix in Office.css. First, ensure the whiteboard container clips cleanly: add 'overflow: hidden; white-space: nowrap;' to the existing '.office-whiteboard' rule. Second, on the wb-vibe-text span: add 'white-space: nowrap; display: block; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis;' to the existing '.wb-vibe-text' rule (the chalk-write animation already uses 'display: inline-block' — change that to 'display: block' with the above overrides, OR keep inline-block and wrap in a block-level container). Also add a mobile media query to reduce the font-size slightly if needed: '@media (max-width: 480px) { .wb-vibe-text { font-size: 0.48rem; letter-spacing: 0.06em; } }'. WHY: The whiteboard is the most prominent text element in the office. 'FL OW' is a broken first impression for every mobile visitor. This is a 3-line CSS fix.

ideas.title: Conference Table Meeting Scene — Someone Finally Sits Down
ideas.description:
The conference table is the most underused element in the office. It has beautiful vibe-reactive chairs that have never hosted a single character. The chairs already outnumber desk workers — they're waiting. Implementation in Office.jsx: when idleWorkers.length >= 2, compute 'const meetingWorkers = idleWorkers.slice(0, Math.min(idleWorkers.length, 3))'. Render a 'meeting-scene' overlay div absolutely positioned over the conference table. Inside it, for each meetingWorker, render a tiny seated avatar: a 16×16 CharacterAvatar at reduced size='16', with CSS class 'char--seated'. The seated avatars should orbit the table — use the existing CHAIR_ANGLES array for positioning. In Office.css add: '.conference-table { position: relative; }' (if not already) and '.char--seated { position: absolute; width: 16px; height: 16px; transform: translate(-50%, -50%); z-index: 3; }' '.char--seated .char__avatar { width: 16px; height: 16px; border-width: 1px; }'. Add a tiny 'meeting-badge' above the table when 2+ workers are seated: '
💬 Huddle
' with CSS: '.conf-meeting-badge { position: absolute; top: -16px; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); font-size: 0.38rem; color: var(--text-muted); white-space: nowrap; letter-spacing: 0.08em; opacity: 0; transition: opacity 0.5s ease; }' '.conference-table[data-meeting] .conf-meeting-badge { opacity: 1; }'. WHY: The conference table is prime real estate in the center of the floor, and right now it's scenery. Seeing a character actually seated there during idle time turns a prop into a narrative — the office is having a huddle, not just pausing.

ideas.title: Full-Sync Aura — The Floor Glows When Everyone Is Locked In
ideas.description:
Carried forward from #37 and #38 — still not implemented. In Office.jsx, directly below the existing nonMgr/workingWorkers/idleWorkers declarations add: 'const isFullSync = nonMgr.length > 0 && idleWorkers.length === 0 && workingWorkers.length === nonMgr.length'. Add 'data-full-sync={isFullSync || undefined}' to the '.office-floor' div alongside the existing data-vibe and data-has-error. Add a tiny banner just below the office-sign div: '{isFullSync &&
⚡ Full sync
}'. In Office.css: '.full-sync-banner { position: absolute; top: 0; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); font-size: 0.42rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.14em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--accent); background: rgba(167,139,250,0.1); border: 1px solid rgba(167,139,250,0.28); border-top: none; border-radius: 0 0 6px 6px; padding: 2px 10px; z-index: 5; white-space: nowrap; animation: banner-drop 0.4s cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1) both; }' '@keyframes banner-drop { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateX(-50%) translateY(-8px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateX(-50%) translateY(0); } }'. Floor border: '.office-floor[data-full-sync] { animation: full-sync-pulse 3.5s ease-in-out infinite; }' '@keyframes full-sync-pulse { 0%, 100% { box-shadow: 0 8px 40px rgba(0,0,0,0.35), inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.05); } 50% { box-shadow: 0 8px 40px rgba(0,0,0,0.35), 0 0 0 3px rgba(167,139,250,0.35), inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.08); } }'. WHY: Every team member working simultaneously is rare and worth celebrating. Right now that perfect moment is invisible. The electric border and banner make it earned.

ideas.title: Away Sign Timer Escalation — 12 Minutes and 2 Hours Are Not the Same
ideas.description: Carried forward from #38 — still not implemented. The AwaySign already receives idleMinutes as a prop. Every sign looks identical — same size, same rotation, same urgency — whether someone stepped out 12 minutes ago or 2 hours ago. Zero visual escalation. In the AwaySign component (Office.jsx), add 'data-tier={idleMinutes >= 60 ? "long" : idleMinutes >= 30 ? "mid" : "short"}' to the outer div. In Office.css add: '.desk-away-sign[data-tier="mid"] .desk-away-sign__note { transform: rotate(-5deg) scale(1.12); filter: saturate(1.3); transition: transform 0.6s ease, filter 0.6s ease; }' '.desk-away-sign[data-tier="long"] .desk-away-sign__note { transform: rotate(-9deg) scale(1.25); filter: saturate(1.6) brightness(1.1); animation: sign-sag 2.5s ease-in-out infinite; }' '@keyframes sign-sag { 0%, 100% { transform: rotate(-9deg) scale(1.25); } 50% { transform: rotate(-6deg) scale(1.25); } }'. For long-tier, also tint the note yellow: '.desk-away-sign[data-tier="long"] .desk-away-sign__note rect:first-child { fill: #fde047 !important; }'. WHY: Ambient storytelling lives in detail. A sign that grows more disheveled and tilted with time tells the story of someone who really hasn't come back. The desk gets visually messier without a single new DOM element.

ideas.title: Vibe Transition Caption Bar — Give the Shift a Voice
ideas.description:
Bloberto's speech bubble fires a transition quote when the vibe changes (e.g. 'in-flow:crushing' key fires a VIBE_TRANSITION_QUOTES lookup). But that bubble is small and attached to one character. The floor itself is silent during a vibe shift. New component: 'VibeCaption'. In Office.jsx add: 'const [vibeCaption, setVibeCaption] = useState(null)'. Add a useEffect watching 'vibe' that fires when vibe changes: 'useEffect(() => { const CAPTIONS = { "crushing": "🚀 Team locked in", "on-fire": "🔥 All hands", "in-flow": "⚡ In the zone", "slow-day": "☁️ Easy does it", "after-hours": "🌙 Burning the midnight oil" }; setVibeCaption(CAPTIONS[vibe] || null); const t = setTimeout(() => setVibeCaption(null), 2800); return () => clearTimeout(t); }, [vibe])'. Render below the conference table area: '{vibeCaption &&
{vibeCaption}
}'. CSS: '.vibe-caption { position: absolute; bottom: 12%; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); font-size: 0.48rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--accent); background: rgba(0,0,0,0.45); backdrop-filter: blur(4px); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08); border-radius: 20px; padding: 3px 14px; z-index: 6; white-space: nowrap; animation: caption-rise 2.8s cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1) forwards; pointer-events: none; }' '@keyframes caption-rise { 0% { opacity: 0; transform: translateX(-50%) translateY(8px); } 15% { opacity: 1; transform: translateX(-50%) translateY(0); } 80% { opacity: 1; } 100% { opacity: 0; transform: translateX(-50%) translateY(-4px); } }'. WHY: Vibe changes are the most dramatic moments in the dashboard. Right now they're announced only by the pill header and shockwave ring. A caption that rises from the floor and fades gives the shift cinematic weight — like a subtitle for the office's emotional state.

ideas.title: Progress Ring 100% Burst — A Completion Worth Celebrating
ideas.description: When worker.progress hits 100%, the progress ring around their avatar turns green (nice), but there's no spatial celebration on the office floor. The Character component already tracks isPinged and can receive extra state. Add in Character component: 'const [justCompleted, setJustCompleted] = useState(false)'. Add 'const prevProgressRef = useRef(worker.progress)'. In a useEffect watching worker.progress: 'useEffect(() => { if (worker.progress >= 100 && prevProgressRef.current < 100) { setJustCompleted(true); const t = setTimeout(() => setJustCompleted(false), 1400); return () => clearTimeout(t); } prevProgressRef.current = worker.progress ?? 0; }, [worker.progress])'. Add 'data-complete={justCompleted || undefined}' to the char div. In Office.css: '.char[data-complete]::after { content: "✅"; position: absolute; top: -4px; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); font-size: 10px; animation: complete-burst 1.4s ease-out forwards; pointer-events: none; z-index: 12; }' '@keyframes complete-burst { 0% { opacity: 1; transform: translateX(-50%) scale(0.5) translateY(0); } 60% { opacity: 1; transform: translateX(-50%) scale(1.3) translateY(-18px); } 100% { opacity: 0; transform: translateX(-50%) scale(1) translateY(-28px); } }'. Also add a brief glow on the avatar: '.char[data-complete] .char__avatar { animation: complete-glow 0.5s ease-out 2 both; }' '@keyframes complete-glow { 0%, 100% { box-shadow: 0 3px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.4); } 50% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 4px rgba(34,197,94,0.5), 0 3px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.4); } }'. WHY: A worker finishing a task is a real event. The progress ring quietly turning green is the office equivalent of a tree falling with no one watching. A burst makes it a moment the whole room can feel.

ideas.title: Vibe Forecast Weather Widget — One Glyph That Reads the Room
ideas.description:
Carried forward from #37 and #38 — still not implemented. Add a tiny weather widget tucked in the lower-right corner just above the cactus. In Office.jsx, after the coffee-corner div and before the door div, add: '
{VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS[vibe]}
'. Near the top of the file define: 'const VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS = { "on-fire": "⛈️", "crushing": "☀️", "in-flow": "🌤️", "slow-day": "🌧️", "after-hours": "🌙" }'. CSS: '.vibe-weather { position: absolute; right: 3%; bottom: 28%; font-size: 0.85rem; line-height: 1; pointer-events: none; z-index: 1; opacity: 0.55; transition: opacity 0.5s ease, transform 0.5s ease; }' '.vibe-weather--on-fire { animation: wx-urgent 1.4s ease-in-out infinite; }' '@keyframes wx-urgent { 0%, 100% { transform: scale(1); } 50% { transform: scale(1.15) rotate(5deg); } }' '.vibe-weather--crushing { animation: wx-sunny 3s ease-in-out infinite; }' '@keyframes wx-sunny { 0%, 100% { transform: scale(1); } 50% { transform: scale(1.08); } }'. WHY: Weather glyphs are a universal language read in under a second. It layers onto the existing vibe system as an ambient second-opinion readout — a tiny oracle in the corner that confirms the room's emotional forecast.

ideas.title: After-Hours Starfield Floor — The Office Becomes the Night Sky
ideas.description: Carried forward from #38 — still not implemented. During after-hours vibe the floor grid already shifts to deep indigo, but it still looks like an office. After-hours is the one vibe where the whole metaphor could shapeshift: the grid fades out and tiny CSS-drawn stars replace it, turning the floor into a cosmic overhead view. Pure CSS, zero DOM changes. In Office.css, add: '.office-floor[data-vibe="after-hours"]::after { content: ""; position: absolute; inset: 0; pointer-events: none; z-index: 0; background-image: radial-gradient(circle 1.5px at 7% 14%, rgba(255,255,255,0.55) 1px, transparent 1px), radial-gradient(circle 1px at 19% 38%, rgba(200,200,255,0.45) 1px, transparent 1px), radial-gradient(circle 1.5px at 34% 9%, rgba(255,255,255,0.6) 1px, transparent 1px), radial-gradient(circle 1px at 52% 22%, rgba(220,210,255,0.5) 1px, transparent 1px), radial-gradient(circle 1px at 68% 47%, rgba(255,255,255,0.4) 1px, transparent 1px), radial-gradient(circle 1.5px at 81% 11%, rgba(255,255,255,0.55) 1px, transparent 1px), radial-gradient(circle 1px at 12% 61%, rgba(200,210,255,0.4) 1px, transparent 1px), radial-gradient(circle 1px at 43% 72%, rgba(255,255,255,0.45) 1px, transparent 1px), radial-gradient(circle 1.5px at 74% 68%, rgba(220,200,255,0.5) 1px, transparent 1px), radial-gradient(circle 1px at 91% 33%, rgba(255,255,255,0.45) 1px, transparent 1px), radial-gradient(circle 1px at 28% 85%, rgba(200,200,255,0.4) 1px, transparent 1px), radial-gradient(circle 1.5px at 60% 90%, rgba(255,255,255,0.5) 1px, transparent 1px); background-size: 100% 100%; animation: starfield-twinkle 4s ease-in-out infinite; }' '@keyframes starfield-twinkle { 0%, 100% { opacity: 0.6; } 50% { opacity: 1; } }'. Also: '.office-floor[data-vibe="after-hours"] { --grid-line: rgba(99,102,241,0.03); }'. WHY: After-hours is the loneliest vibe — the one where nobody should be there. It deserves a visual identity that breaks the office metaphor entirely. The floor stops being a floor and becomes a universe to stare into.

ideas.title: Ghost Desk Dust Aura — Abandoned Desks Collect Memories
ideas.description: Ghost workers (OOO) already have a faded, translucent desk style via '.desk--ghost'. But the desk is just visually dimmed — it doesn't feel abandoned, just inactive. What if a ghost desk slowly accumulates a subtle dust-particle aura? No new JSX needed. In Office.css, add a ghost desk ambient particle effect: '.desk--ghost::before { content: ""; position: absolute; inset: -4px; border-radius: 8px; background: radial-gradient(ellipse 80% 60% at 50% 110%, rgba(148,163,184,0.12), transparent 70%); animation: ghost-dust 5s ease-in-out infinite; pointer-events: none; z-index: 0; }' '@keyframes ghost-dust { 0%, 100% { opacity: 0.4; transform: scale(1); } 50% { opacity: 0.8; transform: scale(1.04); } }'. Additionally, add a tiny '💨' or '…' text that occasionally floats up using a separate after pseudo-element: '.desk--ghost::after { content: "·  ·  ·"; position: absolute; top: -10px; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); font-size: 0.4rem; color: rgba(148,163,184,0.35); letter-spacing: 0.3em; animation: desk-dust-float 7s ease-in-out infinite; pointer-events: none; }' '@keyframes desk-dust-float { 0%, 100% { opacity: 0; transform: translateX(-50%) translateY(0); } 40% { opacity: 0.7; transform: translateX(-50%) translateY(-6px); } 80% { opacity: 0; transform: translateX(-50%) translateY(-10px); } }'. WHY: A ghost desk is the office's way of saying 'someone used to sit here.' The dust aura makes absence visible and atmospheric — it turns OOO from an absence of presence into a presence of absence. That's the kind of storytelling that makes people stare.
